title: Copper Dragonborn
entryLore: Descended from the witty and prank-loving copper dragons of the rocky hills, these dragonborn carry the power of acid in their breath. They often possess a sharp mind and a playful spirit, reflecting the social and often humorous nature of their draconic kin.

lore.typicalDwelling: Those of copper draconic ancestry often dwell in the sun-warmed reaches of rocky hills, winding canyons, or complex underground warrens. Their homes are designed with a focus on hospitality and entertainment, featuring intricate acoustic properties, hidden corridors for harmless pranks, and comfortable galleries for sharing stories and songs. They value architectural cleverness and social utility, their settlements often feeling more like vibrant social hubs than fortresses.
lore.typicalAttire: Copper dragonborn favor attire that is both durable and expressive, reflecting their energetic and witty nature. They wear oil-treated leathers, heavy fabrics in shades of reddish-brown, deep sienna, and burnished copper, often decorated with intricate patterns and small, jingling charms. Their clothing is designed to be comfortable for both climbing and social gatherings. Many wear sashes and belts with many compartments for carrying curiosities and musical instruments.
lore.typicalEnvironment: Arid hills, canyon-lands, and any region that rewards wit, agility, and the pursuit of social interaction are the copper dragonborn's preferred habitats. They thrive in environments where they can utilize their natural agility and their love for complex terrain. Many serve as specialized messengers, professional entertainers, scouts in rugged terrain, or mediators between neighboring communities.
lore.societyType: Copper dragonborn society is informal and organized around shared humor and the joy of intellectual pursuit. They value wit, creativity, and the preservation of oral traditions. Leadership is often held by those who are the most persuasive speakers or the most creative problem-solvers. Their culture is one of collective levity and the belief that a good laugh is the best defense against the hardships of life.
lore.typicalAssociation: Copper dragonborn tend toward chaotic good alignments, driven by a deep-seated appreciation for personal freedom and a commitment to spreading joy. They are a gregarious and benevolent people, though their constant jokes can sometimes be exhausting for more serious races. They are fiercely protective of their friends and will go to great lengths to help those in need, especially if they can do so with a bit of flair.
lore.physicalAppearance: Copper dragonborn possess a lithe and exceptionally agile appearance, standing 6 to 6.5 feet tall with lean, athletic builds. Their scales are a vibrant shade of reddish-brown or burnished copper, often with a faint, metallic luster that catches the light. Their most striking feature is their wide, wing-like ears and a pair of long, backward-curving horns. Their eyes are large and bright, in shades of turquoise, green, or vivid amber. Their faces are characterized by a refined, toothy muzzle and a powerful jaw that seems almost always on the verge of a witty remark. They move with a springy, restless grace that conveys both quick reflexes and an irrepressible energy.

traits.name: Breath Weapon
traits.description: When you take the [[attack|Attack]] action, you can replace one of your attacks with an exhalation of [[acid_damage|Acid damage]] in a 15-foot cone or a 30-foot line (your choice). The [[saving_throw|Saving Throw]] DC is 8 + your Constitution modifier + your [[proficiency_bonus|Proficiency Bonus]]. On a failed save, a creature takes 1d10 damage, or half as much on a successful one. The damage increases by 1d10 at levels 5, 11, and 17. You can use this a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus per [[long_rest|Long Rest]].

traits.name: Draconic Flight
traits.description: Starting at Level 5, you can use a [[bonus_action|Bonus Action]] to sprout spectral wings for 10 minutes or until you're [[incapacitated_condition|Incapacitated]]. For the duration, you have a [[fly_speed|Flying Speed]] equal to your walking speed. Once you use this trait, you can't do so again until you finish a [[long_rest|Long Rest]].
